subject: Sociology
title: Experiments in Sociology
description: A fast breakdown of Experiments in Sociology for builders.

hook.text: Researchers sent identical resumes with different names. Jamal got fewer callbacks than James. That's an experiment.

buildup.text: Experiments test causation by changing one variable and holding others constant. They're rare but powerful in sociology.

discovery.text: Field experiments happen in real settings. The resume study revealed hiring discrimination with hard numbers.

twist.text: Lab experiments control more but feel artificial. People behave differently when they know they're being watched.

climax.text: Ethical limits restrict what sociologists can test. You can't randomly assign people to poverty to study its effects.

punchline.text: Sometimes the simplest test reveals the deepest bias.

quiz.question: What did the resume experiment demonstrate?
options: That all employers are equally fair
That lab experiments are better than field experiments
